input:
Hospitalization report
This is the patient BENDJELIDA MOHAMED, aged 09, originally from and residing in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, followed at our level since December 2011 for cystic fibrosis with double respiratory and digestive phenotypic expression on the homozygous genetic mutation. F508del; Admitted to our department on 03/24/2026 for an exacerbation of his illness. HISTORY:
Family:
-Father: aged 62 in BES. -Mother: aged 42; in BES. - Consanguinity: yes
-Siblings: Sister aged 14 known on duty for cystic fibrosis
Child died at 03 days of life due to lack of meconium emission. Rest of siblings in good health with negative sweat test. Personal:
Physiological
-Pregnancy: Well monitored
-Term birth by vaginal route. -Apgar 7/10 then 09/10
-Birth weight: 02Kg 900, Height (51 cm), BW (34 cm)
-Meconium emitted >24 hours, no notion of neonatal jaundice
- Breastfeeding: Exclusive maternal until the age of 4 months then artificial
-Diversification started at the age of 6 months
-Vaccination: CORRECT
-Psychomotor development: GOOD
-Schooling: 4th AP. Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestic:
Concept of meconium emission delay
Death of a brother at the age of 3 days (no meconium emission). Sister aged 14 known on duty for cystic fibrosis. Clinic:
Delayed meconium emission. Chronic wet cough. Chronic oily diarrhea. Paraclinic:
-Sweat test: positive twice. 1st Test on 06/20/2017 (Right forearm) 394 mg (Weight of sweat) /103 meq/l (Chlorine level)
02nd Test 07/09/2017 (Right forearm) 393 mg (Weight of sweat) /96 meq/l (Chlorine level). -Genetic study: 01/31/2012: homozygous. F508del
-Fecal elastase: 03/26/2026: 0.6ug Exocrine pancreatic insufficiency
Processing in progress:
-For respiratory purposes:
Respiratory physiotherapy at home twice daily
Hypertonic nebulization
Nebulization with Colistin every other month
- For nutritional purposes:
Creon 12000 IU (520 IU/Kg)
Alvityl 1 tsp/day
Vit K 10 mg/15 days
Vit D amp/3 months
Vit E 1cp/d
Therapeutic compliance: Good
HISTORY OF ILLNESS in relation to the reason for hospitalization: 03/24/2026
The onset of the signs dates back 15 days previously with accentuation of the cough becoming bothersome, change in the sputum which has become greenish and thick, with asthenia and decreased appetite. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 03/24/2026
• Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 23 kg (-1.41 DS). Size: 125 CM (-1.26 DS). BMI: 14.72 Kg/m2 (-0.93DS). P/A: 83%; P/T: 94%. Weight-loss eutrophy. • Average general condition, febrile: T: 40.1° C, mild malnutrition. • Skin examination: Pale skin, no jaundice or hemorrhagic syndrome. • ENT examination: clean throat, nasal obstruction. • Pleuropulmonary examination: FR: 22c/, fits of wet cough, auscultation, bilateral rumbling rales, vesicular murmurs well transmitted in 2 pulmonary fields. - Absence of signs of struggle, Spo2: 98% under AA. - Signs of chronic respiratory failure (clubbing)
• Cardiovascular examination: HR: 92 b/min. Auscultation: no breathing or BSA, heart sounds were clearly audible. • Examination of the abdomen, distended soft abdomen, 01 to 02 soft fatty stools, no signs of dehydration, free hernia orifices, no rectal prolapse. • Examination of other devices without particularity. PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION:
CBC: correct: WBC: 6500 ele/mm3: PNN: 2470. Lymphocyte: 3100
HB: 14.7g/dl; VGM: 86.4fl; CCMH: 34.5g/dl
PLQ: 337,000 el/mm 3. TP: 75.5% TQ: 15 Sec TCK: 35 Sec
CRP: Negative PCT: < 0.05 ng/ml (negative)
Blood ionogram: correct: Na: 140 mmol/l K: 3.84 mmol/l Cl: 103 mmol/l
ASAT: 22U/l gGT: 11.28 LDH: 573 U/L
Blood sugar: 0.87 g/L TG: 1.25 g/l
Magnesemia: 21.95 mg/L
Albumin: 40.4g/l Creat: 5.02 mg/l Urea: 0.11 g/l Protein Total: 69.3 g/l
Ferritin: 20.08 ng/ml 25 OH Vit D: 13.1 ng/ml (vitamin D insufficiency)
Support:
On the respiratory level
• Nebulization with SSH before respiratory physiotherapy. • Twice-daily respiratory physiotherapy
• IV Bi Antibiotic Therapy:
• Fortum 200mg/kg/day. • Amikacin 25 mg/kg/day IV for 10 days. • Zomax 1 day/2 days
Nutritionally:
• Interview with the dietician.
• Growth curve. • polyvitamin supplementation: {vit D/2 months Vit K/15 days Vit E/15 days} and iron deficiency. • Creon 12000 U 01 capsule 10/day
Evolution during hospitalization:
Regression of signs of struggle, complete disappearance of cough with reduction in sputum volume. The child stayed for 13 days having received Bi antibiotic therapy: Fortum for 13 days, Amikacin for 10 days with venofer infusion 3 times at 3 day intervals. OUTLINE AND FOCUS OF THE DISEASE:
On the respiratory level:
• Sign of chronic respiratory failure “digital clubbing”
• no sign of chronic cor pulmonale. Heart echo (05/04//2026): without anomaly
• EFR (01-12-2024):
Parameter Result interpretation
FEV 90% normal
FEV/FVC 110% normal (no obstructive syndrome)
FVC 81% moderate decrease (slight restriction)
DEP 2.89L/sec slightly decreased. • ECBC of sputum: 03/29/2026: Absence of germ
Arterial blood gas: 06/16/25
Ph: 7.324 in2:91 mm hg pco2 39.3 mm hg without abnormality. • Chest CT 02/24/2025: bronchial syndrome made of thickening of the proximal bronchial walls without bronchiectasis associated with mediatisnal and right hilar adenomegaly. On the cardiac and hemodynamic level:
• no signs of heart failure (no edema or hepatomegaly, or jugular turgidity). • no signs of chronic cor pulmonale. • heart echo 04/05/2026: Without anomalies. On the digestive and hepatobiliary level:
• No jaundice, no notion of digestive bleeding, absence of hepatomegaly. Absence of rectal prolapse. Absence of constipation, no sign of intussusception. • Liver assessment (03/24/2026) correct. ASAT: 22U/l. gGT: 11.28. LDH: 573 U/L. • Abdominopelvic ultrasound 03/2025: homogeneous hepatomegaly, discreet left pyelic ectasia. Nutritionally and metabolically:
• Weight: 23 kg (-1.41 DS). Size: 125 CM (-1.26 DS). BMI: 14.72 Kg/m2 (-0.93DS). P/A: 83%; P/T: 94%. Weight-loss eutrophy
• Daily ration preserved. Blood sugar: 0.83 g/l HbA1C (12/24/2025): 5.51% (normal). • Correct nutritional assessment except hypovitaminosis D (03/24/2026): TG: 1.25 g/l
Magnesemia: 21.95 mg/L. Albumin: 40.4g/l. Creat: 5.02 mg/l. Urea: 0.11 g/l. Total Protein: 69.3 g/l. Ferritin: 20.08 ng/ml. 25 OH Vit D: 13.1 ng/ml (vitamin D insufficiency). On the ENT level:
• Rhinitis with nasal obstruction. CT of the sinuses 03/23/2026: Pan sinusitis
• Nasofibroscopy: 15/12/2025:
Cavum: Adenoid-like hypertrophy of the non-obstructive roof. Pharyngolarynx: Free, Mobile vocal cords not edematous
Nasal cavity: Allergic-looking rhinitis with hypertrophy of the turbinates, abundant clear mucous secretion on both sides, small polyp of the middle meatus. ENT opinion: Chronic rhino sinusitis is almost permanent in cystic fibrosis with sometimes appearance of polyps, benefit of antihistamine treatment and local corticosteroids during periods of crisis, especially in winter. CONCLUSION:
The child Bendjlida Mohamed aged 09, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed since the age of 3 months for cystic fibrosis with dual phenotypic respiratory and digestive expression with homozygous genetic mutation. F508del, readmitted for moderate exacerbation of his illness having progressed well after dual IV antibiotic therapy. Child discharged after good clinical and biological evolution. Inspection appointment on: 07/07/2026
To collect / To do:
- Abdominopelvic ultrasound
- Thoracic CT with injection
- ECBC with antibiogram. - Plan a nighttime saturation curve for the next hospitalization
- Referral to ENT
Resident of the department: Dr Djoudi Assistant of the department: Dr OUSSALAH
